description: The Musitano crime family is a 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, with branches in Italy. Founded by Angelo Musitano in the 1940s, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, and murder. The family was involved in a power struggle with rival Hamilton families, leading to the killings of Johnny Papalia and Carmen Barillaro in 1997. Key members Angelo and Pat Musitano were killed in 2017 and 2020, respectively.

country_links.evidence_quote: another branch of it is active in Platì in Southern Italy and Buccinasco and Bareggio in Northern Italy
country_links.context: The organization has an active branch in Platì, Buccinasco, and Bareggio, Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Michael Cudmore and Daniel Tomassetti who had both fled to Mexico in May 2017 and January 2018 respectively
country_links.context: Members of the Musitano crime family fled to Mexico to evade arrest.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Musitano brothers had also been fed up with being a satellite (crew) of the Buffalo family
country_links.context: The Musitano family had a satellite relationship with the Buffalo crime family in the United States.
